analysis.reasoning: Core message: A neutral/positive report that Utah elementary school pupils wrote to President Xi for Chinese New Year, expressing affection for China and its culture, and sharing progress in learning Chinese. Evaluation per narrative: CH1 (West immoral/hostile) – Not present; no criticism of the West. CH2 (China benevolent power) – Not explicitly; no claims about international conduct or multilateralism. CH3 (epic history) – Not mentioned. CH4 (appropriate political system/values) – Not addressed; no reference to governance or values of China's system. CH5 (CCP government succeeds) – Not present; no mention of government achievements. CH6 (China’s culture, nature, heritage interesting) – Yes, implicit promotion of Chinese culture and affection toward it and learning Chinese fits cultural appreciation. Conclusion: Only CH6 applies.
